title: approvals — outbound webhooks
description: This module dispatches a security-relevant + compliance-relevant subset of approval lifecycle events as outbound webhooks for tenants that opt in (SIEM integrations, custom governance pipelines, on-prem audit ingestors). Payloads are minimally-redacted — approver + requester fingerprints (SHA-256 of user_id), action metadata, timestamps. No PII, no context_json field values, no decision comments. Tenants configure endpoint URLs + secrets via the notifications module's outbound-webhook subscription surface.

outbound_webhook_kinds.notes: Sanitised for SIEM ingest. Tenant policies that need a per-request full-fidelity feed use the internal audit-report endpoint instead (authenticated + rate-limited).

payload.delegated_from_fingerprint: string | null (SHA-256 of delegated_from_id when the decider was acting under a delegation)

outbound_webhook_kinds.notes: The rejection reason (approval_decisions.comment) is NEVER included in the webhook — comments can carry internal-tier context that shouldn't cross the SIEM boundary. Consumers who need reasons pull them through the authenticated /approvals/instances/{id} endpoint.
P0-priority — this is the compliance-critical event marking when tenant state actually changed. SOC 2 auditors treat this webhook as evidence of change-control enforcement.

payload.note: P0 security signal — tenant SIEM MUST trigger investigation

outbound_webhook_kinds.notes: Fingerprints intentionally omitted here — a forgery attempt should trigger a full-fidelity manual investigation, not surface an attacker's identity via a payload. The internal audit log holds the specifics.

rationale: Approval-lifecycle webhooks are appropriate at this level — the payload contains only fingerprints + coarse event metadata. No PII, no context_json field values, no decision comments.

ApprovalTemplate* events are deliberately NOT webhooked — template edits are governance-configuration changes; tenants pulling those via a webhook subscribe to the tenant.{id}.approvals broadcast channel instead.
Every webhook is signed via HMAC-SHA256 with the tenant's shared secret (rotated via the notifications module's endpoint-management UI).
